[[File:Dorgeshuun crossbow detail.png|left|120px]]
The '''Dorgeshuun crossbow''' is a [[Crossbow (weapon)|crossbow]] requiring level 28 [[Ranged]] to equip. It is often used to train [[Ranged]] at low to medium levels, due to it having several advantages over similar [[crossbow (weapon)|crossbow]]s with the same level requirements. It has the same attack bonus as an [[iron crossbow]]. It has an attack speed that is one [[tick]] faster than most other crossbows, and it is able to fire [[bone bolt]]s, which are significantly cheaper than most other ammunition of similar strength. The fact it is a crossbow also gives it an advantage over [[shortbow]]s, allowing the use of the [[shield slot]] for defensive or offensive purposes. Despite the bone bolts being roughly equivalent to iron bolts in ranged strength, the Dorgeshuun crossbow's faster attack speed means that its damage-per-second output when firing bone bolts is comparable to that of a regular crossbow firing steel or even mithril bolts (depending on the player's exact Ranged level).

It can be bought from [[Nardok]] in the [[Dorgesh-Kaan mine|Dorgeshuun Mines]] after the completion of [[The Lost Tribe]] quest. Completion of this quest is not required to equip it, however, meaning it can be purchased from other players and used without completing this quest. It can fire metal [[bolts]] up to iron, except [[silver bolts]], and is the only crossbow that can use [[bone bolt]]s. Note, however, that bolts containing unique tips and their (e) variants are not able to be fired from it.

This crossbow is the only [[crossbow (weapon)|crossbow]] with an [[attack speed]] of 5. This makes it 1 [[tick]] faster than most crossbows - it is only slower than the [[Karil's crossbow]], the [[hunters' crossbow]], and its upgraded variant, the [[Hunters' sunlight crossbow|hunter's sunlight crossbow]], which all have an attack speed of 4. It should be noted that the Karil's crossbow is two-handed.

Weighing only 2.267kg, it is also the lightest crossbow in the game, making it ideal to use along with the [[Mith grapple|mithril grapple]] when conservation of [[run energy]] is necessary, such as in the [[Hallowed Sepulchre]].

==Special attack==
[[File:Snipe.gif|thumb|250px|A player performs the Dorgeshuun crossbow's special attack, ''Snipe''.]]
The Dorgeshuun crossbow has a [[special attack]], ''Snipe'' (75%), which will never miss as long as the target has not been damaged previously or you weren't the last one to damage the target.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/1138455869358837764|date=11 June 2019|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/100366|archivedate=29 July 2020|quote=[Does the Dorgeshuun crossbow's spec reduce the target's defence level even if it's not an "unsuspecting target"? Also, what is an unsuspecting target?] If the accuracy roll is passed, yes. The definition of "unsuspecting target" is "target has not been hit before" OR "target was last hit by someone other than me".}} It lowers the target's [[Defence]] by the amount of damage dealt, similar to the [[Bandos godsword]]'s and [[bone dagger]]'s special attacks. The defence lowering is only applied when the target doesn't currently have their defence levels lowered.<ref>{{PlainCiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://x.com/JagexAsh/status/1798626951885889739|date=8 June 2024|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/tsLWH|archivedate=6 June 2024|quote=Correct - they'll only try to reduce it if it's on (or above) its base level at the time.
If it had been reduced previously, then restored to the base level, they could try to reduce it again.}}</ref>

The special attack requires completion of the [[quest]] [[Death to the Dorgeshuun]].

==Trivia==
* A code comment attached to the implementation of the Dorgeshuun crossbow's special attack mentions "double damage" being involved in some form or other; no such mechanic is known to exist in practice.{{CiteTwitter|author=Mod Ash|url=https://twitter.com/JagexAsh/status/523425915582545920|date=18 October 2014|archiveurl=https://reldo.runescape.wiki/i/104800|archivedate=27 April 2023|quote=Btw, the comments in the code say something about double damage, but I don't think it actually doubles anything.}}
